control_text: AC-03: Access Enforcement. Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.
chunk_text: The system enforces role-based access control (RBAC) for all logical access to information resources. Users are granted access based on their assigned roles, and access permissions are validated at each point of entry using centralized authentication services.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes RBAC enforcement for logical access.

control_text: AU-02: Event Logging. Identify the types of events that the system is capable of logging in support of the audit function.
chunk_text: The system is configured to log successful and failed authentication attempts, privilege escalation events, file access to sensitive data, configuration changes, and administrative actions. The list of auditable events is reviewed annually.
rationale: Direct match: chunk identifies specific auditable event types.

control_text: SC-28: Protection of Information at Rest. Protect the confidentiality of information at rest.
chunk_text: All sensitive data stored on servers, databases, and endpoint devices is encrypted at rest using AES-256 encryption. Full disk encryption is enabled on all laptops and mobile devices. Encryption keys are managed centrally through the key management system.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes encryption of data at rest.

control_text: IR-04: Incident Handling. Implement an incident handling capability for incidents that includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.
chunk_text: The incident response team implements a structured incident handling process that includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident activity phases. All incidents are tracked through the ticketing system.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes full incident handling lifecycle.

control_text: IA-05: Authenticator Management. Manage system authenticators by establishing initial authenticator content, ensuring authenticators have sufficient strength, and changing default authenticators prior to first use.
chunk_text: Authenticator management procedures require passwords to be at least 14 characters with complexity requirements. Passwords expire every 90 days and the last 24 passwords are remembered. Hardware tokens are inventoried and revoked upon personnel termination.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes authenticator strength and lifecycle.

control_text: CP-09: System Backup. Conduct backups of user-level information and system-level information. Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of backup information.
chunk_text: Full system backups are performed weekly and incremental backups are performed nightly. Backup media is encrypted using AES-256 and stored at a geographically separate offsite facility. Backup restoration is tested quarterly to verify data integrity.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes backup schedule, encryption, and testing.

control_text: CM-06: Configuration Settings. Establish and document configuration settings for components that reflect the most restrictive mode consistent with operational requirements.
chunk_text: Systems are deployed with hardened configurations based on CIS Benchmarks. Configuration settings include disabling unnecessary services, enforcing strong cipher suites, and implementing recommended registry and kernel parameters.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes hardened configuration settings.

control_text: PE-03: Physical Access Control. Enforce physical access authorizations at entry/exit points. Escort visitors and control visitor activity.
chunk_text: Building entry points are controlled by badge readers and monitored by security cameras. Visitors must sign in at reception and be escorted at all times. Server rooms require two-factor authentication (badge plus biometric) for entry.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes physical access controls and visitor management.

control_text: RA-05: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning. Monitor and scan for vulnerabilities in the system and hosted applications. Remediate legitimate vulnerabilities in accordance with an organizational assessment of risk.
chunk_text: Vulnerability scans are conducted weekly on all networked systems using an approved scanning tool. Critical vulnerabilities must be remediated within 15 days, high within 30 days, and medium within 90 days. Scan results are reported to system owners.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes vulnerability scanning and remediation timelines.

control_text: AC-07: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts. Enforce a limit of consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user and automatically lock the account when the maximum number of unsuccessful attempts is exceeded.
chunk_text: After five consecutive failed login attempts, the user account is automatically locked for 30 minutes. The security team is notified of repeated lockouts, and persistent failed attempts trigger an investigation by the incident response team.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes logon attempt limits and lockout policy.

control_text: AC-01: Policy and Procedures. Review and update the current access control policy and procedures.
chunk_text: User access rights must be reviewed at least every six months to ensure that appropriate privileges are maintained. The review covers all active directory accounts and application-level permissions.
rationale: Subject mismatch: control is about reviewing the ACCESS CONTROL POLICY document, chunk is about reviewing individual USER ACCESS RIGHTS.

control_text: AT-01: Policy and Procedures. Review and update the current awareness and training policy and procedures.
chunk_text: All employees complete annual security awareness training covering phishing identification, password hygiene, social engineering tactics, and data handling procedures.
rationale: Subject mismatch: control is about reviewing the TRAINING POLICY document, chunk is about COMPLETING training.

control_text: SI-01: Policy and Procedures. Develop, document, and disseminate the system and information integrity policy and procedures.
chunk_text: The organization monitors information systems using intrusion detection systems, security information and event management (SIEM) tools, and endpoint detection and response (EDR) agents.
rationale: Subject mismatch: control is about DOCUMENTING an integrity policy, chunk describes MONITORING activities.

control_text: CM-01: Policy and Procedures. Review and update the current configuration management policy and procedures.
chunk_text: A baseline configuration is documented for each system type including approved operating system versions, installed software packages, and security configuration settings.
rationale: Subject mismatch: control is about reviewing the CM POLICY, chunk describes a specific BASELINE CONFIGURATION.

control_text: CA-01: Policy and Procedures. Develop, document, and disseminate the assessment, authorization, and monitoring policy and procedures.
chunk_text: Security controls are assessed annually using a combination of automated scanning tools, manual testing, and interviews with system operators.
rationale: Subject mismatch: control is about the ASSESSMENT POLICY document, chunk describes actual ASSESSMENT ACTIVITIES.

control_text: SA-01: Policy and Procedures. Develop the system and services acquisition policy. Designate an official to manage the development and dissemination of the policy.
chunk_text: Cryptographic keys are generated, distributed, stored, and destroyed following documented key management procedures. Keys are stored in FIPS 140-2 validated hardware security modules.
rationale: Cross-family: control is about ACQUISITION policy, chunk is about KEY MANAGEMENT (SC-12 domain).

control_text: AC-04: Information Flow Enforcement. Enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the system and between connected systems.
chunk_text: All employees are required to report suspected security incidents to the helpdesk or security team within one hour of discovery.
rationale: Cross-family: control is about INFORMATION FLOW, chunk is about INCIDENT REPORTING (IR domain).

control_text: AU-12: Audit Record Generation. Provide audit record generation capability for the event types the system is capable of auditing.
chunk_text: The organization deploys firewalls, intrusion detection systems, and web application firewalls at managed network boundaries. All inbound and outbound traffic is filtered.
rationale: Cross-family: control is about AUDIT RECORD GENERATION, chunk is about BOUNDARY PROTECTION (SC domain).

control_text: IA-02: Identification and Authentication. Uniquely identify and authenticate organizational users and associate that unique identification with processes.
chunk_text: Media protection policies define handling requirements for digital and non-digital media containing sensitive information. Media is classified according to the data it contains.
rationale: Cross-family: control is about USER IDENTIFICATION, chunk is about MEDIA PROTECTION (MP domain).

control_text: CP-02: Contingency Plan. Develop a contingency plan that identifies essential missions and business functions, recovery objectives, and restoration priorities.
chunk_text: The organization implements FIPS-validated cryptographic modules for all encryption operations. AES-256 is used for data at rest, TLS 1.3 for data in transit.
rationale: Cross-family: control is about CONTINGENCY PLANNING, chunk is about CRYPTOGRAPHY (SC domain).

control_text: SC-07: Boundary Protection. Monitor and control communications at the external managed interfaces to the system.
chunk_text: System maintenance policies require that all maintenance activities be scheduled, documented, and performed by authorized personnel. Remote maintenance sessions require multi-factor authentication.
rationale: Subject mismatch: control is about BOUNDARY PROTECTION, chunk is about SYSTEM MAINTENANCE.

control_text: AC-06: Least Privilege. Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users that are necessary to accomplish assigned organizational tasks.
chunk_text: Separation of duties is enforced for critical business functions. No single individual can authorize, process, and record a financial transaction.
rationale: Subject mismatch: control is about LEAST PRIVILEGE, chunk is about SEPARATION OF DUTIES (AC-05). Related but different controls.

control_text: AU-06: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting. Review and analyze system audit records for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity.
chunk_text: Each audit record includes the timestamp, source IP address, user identity, event type, event outcome (success or failure), and the affected resource.
rationale: Subject mismatch: control is about REVIEWING audit records, chunk is about audit record CONTENT (AU-03 domain).

control_text: AC-02: Account Management. Define and document the types of accounts allowed and specifically prohibited for use within the system.
chunk_text: This policy has been approved by the Chief Information Security Officer on January 15, 2024. It supersedes all previous versions and is effective immediately upon publication.
rationale: Boilerplate: approval block with no substantive security content.

control_text: IR-01: Policy and Procedures. Develop, document, and disseminate the incident response policy and procedures.
chunk_text: This policy applies to all information systems, personnel, and third-party contractors operating within or on behalf of the organization. The purpose of this document is to establish the requirements for incident management.
rationale: Boilerplate: scope/purpose statement with no prescriptive controls.

control_text: SC-12: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management. Establish and manage cryptographic keys in accordance with key management requirements.
chunk_text: Cryptographic keys are generated, distributed, stored, and destroyed following documented key management procedures. Keys are stored in FIPS 140-2 validated hardware security modules. Key rotation occurs annually or upon suspected compromise.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes key lifecycle management.

control_text: MP-06: Media Sanitization. Sanitize system media prior to disposal, release out of organizational control, or release for reuse.
chunk_text: All storage media is sanitized using NIST SP 800-88 approved methods before disposal or reuse. Hard drives are degaussed and physically destroyed. Sanitization activities are documented and certificates of destruction are retained.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes media sanitization methods.

control_text: AC-17: Remote Access. Establish and document usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of remote access allowed.
chunk_text: Remote access to organizational systems is permitted only through the approved VPN solution using multi-factor authentication. Split tunneling is disabled, and all remote sessions are logged. Remote access agreements must be signed by all telecommuting personnel.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes remote access controls and requirements.

control_text: CA-07: Continuous Monitoring. Develop a system-level continuous monitoring strategy that includes establishing system-level metrics and ongoing control assessments.
chunk_text: The organization employs a continuous monitoring strategy that includes automated vulnerability scanning on a weekly basis, configuration compliance checks daily, and real-time monitoring of security-relevant events through the SIEM platform.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes continuous monitoring strategy and activities.

control_text: AT-02: Literacy Training and Awareness. Provide security and privacy literacy training to system users including managers, senior executives, and contractors.
chunk_text: All employees complete annual security awareness training covering phishing identification, password hygiene, social engineering tactics, and data handling procedures. Role-based training is provided for IT staff, developers, and managers with additional modules.
rationale: Direct match: chunk describes comprehensive security awareness training program.
